NLC INDIA : GHATAMPUR THERMAL PROJECT FULLY COMMISSIONED ⚡🏭

• 🔥 Unit-3 (660 MW) of the Ghatampur Thermal Power Project has achieved Commercial Operation Date (COD) effective June 13, 2026

• 🤝 Project developed by Neyveli Uttar Pradesh Power Ltd (NUPPL), a JV between NLC India (51%) and UPRVUNL (49%)

• 🏗️ Project will strengthen reliable power supply to Uttar Pradesh and support rising national energy demand

🟢 Impact: Positive (Major capacity addition completed, boosting generation capacity, operational revenues and long-term earnings visibility)

ANTHROPIC SHUTS ACCESS TO FABLE 5 & MYTHOS 5 AI MODELS 🚫🤖

• 🇺🇸 Anthropic has disabled access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models following a U.S. government export-control directive

• 🌍 Restriction applies to all foreign nationals, both inside and outside the United States

• ⚠️ Anthropic chose a complete shutdown instead of selective blocking, citing operational complexity

• 🔐 Company said the action stems from concerns over a cybersecurity-related jailbreak vulnerability

• 📝 Anthropic disagreed with the government's assessment, stating similar capabilities are available in other public AI models

• 🔄 Access to other Anthropic AI models remains unaffected

• ⏳ Company expects to provide more details within 24 hours and is working to restore access

- Users can view prices across multiple dates simultaneously to find the cheapest travel days.

India is adding solar faster than it can plug it in.

~45 GW of solar & wind is set to come online in FY27. Roughly 20 GW could sit idle for 4+ months  fully built, but with nowhere to send the power.

The plants are ready. The wires aren't.

Rajasthan alone has 12+ GW stranded in connectivity queues. Gujarat and the South add another ~8 GW.

This isn't a generation problem. It's a transmission problem and it's becoming structural.

India's investment rate is expected to go from 34.6% of GDP today to 37.5% by FY30.

Last time such a capex cycle was seen in 2004-08 period but the nature of capex this time is completely different 

And one can divide this into 3 buckets - 

1) Well-Entrenched (money already flowing): Coal, Renewables, Transmission, Defence, Railways, Oil & Gas

2) Uptick Evidence (gaining momentum): Data Centers, Semis, Green Equipment, BESS

3) Emerging (early innings, high optionality): Nuclear, Water, Shipbuilding, Coal Gasification, Carbon Capture

Coal is not a legacy constraint. India hit a peak demand record of 271 GW on May 21, 2026 for the 4th consecutive all-time high. Thermal anchored the grid at ~63% of generation. At night, ~190 GW of coal ran at near-full capacity with shortages still touching 5.4 GW. NTPC is adding 13 GW. BHEL and L&T are reporting record order inflows.

India added a record 44.6 GW solar + 6 GW wind in FY26. Impressive. But CERC's new Deviation Settlement Mechanism (Apr-26) is quietly ending the "Must Run" free pass for RE generators. By 2031, solar/wind will face the same grid scheduling penalties as coal plants. Standalone RE is dead. RE + Storage is the only viable model going forward. Every new project will need BESS embedded.

BESS could be the defining investment theme of India over the next decade. Battery costs down 70% in 3 years. 117 GWh tendered cumulatively. CEA targets 80 GW by FY36. India is still 90% import-dependent on China for the battery value chain. One needs to check up on how the value chain gets created. 

The HVDC cycle is just starting. RE generation is concentrated (Rajasthan: 119 GW, South India: 106 GW). Demand is everywhere else.  ±800 kV HVDC = 6 GW bulk transfer over 1,000–2,000 km. India needs 120–130 GW across ~22 corridors. Current installed: ~18 GW. That's a 6x scale-up. Combined CEA + Brahmaputra Basin capex = Rs14.3 trillion in transmission alone. GE Vernova, Hitachi Energy, Siemens Energy sit at the centre of this (Around them is a big value chain that is there). India has no fully integrated domestic HVDC OEM yet.

Nuclear  the structural shift is real this time. SHANTI Act passed. 60-year state monopoly dismantled. FDI up to 49% allowed. PFBR achieved first criticality in April 2026 India entered Stage 2 of its nuclear programme. Target: 8.8 GW today → 100 GW by 2047. ~$200bn cumulative opportunity. Maharashtra alone signed nuclear MoUs worth Rs6.5 trillion with Reliance, NTPC, Adani, and others.
L&T targeting 3x nuclear revenue in 5 years. BHEL can currently supply 30–40% of a nuclear plant's bill of materials.

Also semiconductor ecosystem is getting created. 6 months ago this was all policy announcements but Micron ATMP inaugurated. Kaynes OSAT running. CG Power scaling. Tata-ASML MoU signed for Dholera fab (28nm–110nm nodes, 50,000 wafers/month). First sellable wafers expected: late 2026. India's semis market: $25–30bn today → $ 110bn by 2030. ISM 2.0 launched.

And then there is the DC boom that is coming to India. India's installed DC capacity: 1.8 GW → 10.5 GW by FY31 (AI: 6.8 GW, non-AI: 3.7 GW). Microsoft: $ 17.5bn. Google-AdaniConneX: $ 15bn. AWS: $ 15.3bn combined. Reliance-Brookfield-Digital Realty JV: $ 11bn. ~$75bn announced in 5 years. Hyperscalers are treating India as a geopolitical hedge which has stable democracy, growing digital market, coal baseload + RE mix. Power systems alone = $ 20bn opportunity for industrials.

This isn't a government-funded capex cycle hoping private capital shows up.

Corporate debt/GDP: 52% (vs 61% pre-pandemic). Industrial credit growth: 15% YoY. Bank NPAs: 2.5% at multi-year lows. Gross FDI: $ 94.5bn in FY26, +17% YoY.

Private capex to grow at ~16% CAGR through FY31. Investment rate peaks at ~37.5% of GDP.

Source - Morgan Stanely

AXISCADES TECHNOLOGIES: APPROVES USD 206.30 MN AEROSPACE BUSINESS TRANSFER TO AKKODIS

• The Board has approved the transfer of its Engineering Services business in the Aerospace industry to Akkodis Group AG and Akkodis India Private Limited.

• The aggregate transaction value is USD 206.30 million and is expected to be completed within 5–6 months.

• The transaction involves an internal restructuring followed by the phased divestment of the relevant subsidiaries to Akkodis over a two-year period after closing.

• The consideration includes an initial tranche of USD 77.70 million, a second tranche of USD 76.02 million, and a potential performance-linked payment of up to USD 52.58 million.

• The company plans to redeploy the proceeds towards technology- and capability-led acquisitions, manufacturing infrastructure in aerospace, defence, ESAI and spacetech, and strengthening its balance sheet for future growth.

Valuing Commoditized Businesses

Ankita Path's Guiding Principle
- Demand exceeds available supply
- Supply cannot catch up quickly
- Demand remains relatively inelastic

Valuation Framework
- Focus on industry supply-demand balance
- Assess capacity addition timelines
- Track demand durability and visibility
- Evaluate pricing power sustainability
- Monitor margin and cash flow cycles

Key Takeaway
- Commodity businesses create outsized value when demand outpaces supply for extended periods and capacity additions remain constrained

• 🚀 Opportunity has emerged after the collapse of the earlier Franco-German FCAS fighter jet program

• 🛡️ Discussions have already taken place at the Defence Minister level

• ⚙️ France is also expected to share advanced fighter jet engine technology with India

• 🎯 Collaboration could significantly strengthen India's future air combat capabilities and indigenous aerospace ecosystem

**Bernstein On India Power Sector**

**Ratings & Target Prices**
- Adani Power: Outperform | TP ₹220 (₹177)
- JSW Energy: Outperform | TP ₹623 (₹575)
- Waaree Energy: Underperform | TP ₹2,539 (₹2,109)

- Bernstein remains constructive on India's long-term power opportunity, with NTPC, Adani Power, JSW Energy and PFC among preferred plays

Stock Fundamental in detail
@Fundamental3

Anupam Rasayan: ETFA Is The Product, Flow Chemistry Is The Asset

- First company globally to commercialize ETFA using continuous flow chemistry
- ETFA is a key fluorinated intermediate used in pharma, life sciences and advanced materials
- Addressable market estimated at $500–600 Mn

Why It Matters
- Fluorination chemistry is complex and difficult to scale
- Requires precise reaction control and high process efficiency
- Small yield improvements can significantly impact profitability

Advantages Of Flow Chemistry
- Better process control
- Higher consistency and reproducibility
- Improved safety
- Easier scale-up
- Lower waste generation
- Better asset utilization
- Potentially higher yields

Strategic Significance
- Demonstrates advanced manufacturing capability
- Creates a platform for future fluorinated molecules
- Process innovation can become a competitive moat
- Opportunity extends beyond a single product launch

Key Takeaway
- ETFA is the immediate opportunity
- Continuous flow chemistry is the long-term value driver
- Success across additional fluorinated products could unlock a larger growth platform for Anupam Rasayan

1100++ Concall Highlights for Q4FY26
@Concalls3

HOEC's Unusual Q4: Negative Revenue, Positive Profit

What Happened?
- Q4 revenue reported at negative ₹194 Cr
- Yet PAT came in at around ₹30 Cr
- Triggered by reversal of an earlier crude oil sale to HPCL

The Background
- HOEC had previously booked ₹300+ Cr revenue from B-80 crude sales
- Payment dispute arose with HPCL
- Crude remained stored in HPCL's refinery tanks
- Sale remained unresolved at year-end

Accounting Impact
- Earlier revenue was derecognized
- Revenue from operations turned negative
- Government royalty/profit petroleum liabilities linked to the sale were also reversed
- Crude oil was reinstated as inventory

Why Profit Stayed Positive
- Inventory adjustment created a large credit under "Changes in Inventory"
- Crude added back to inventory at significantly higher value
- Inventory gain offset negative revenue impact
- Result: Negative revenue but positive profitability

Key Takeaway
- The quarter reflects an accounting reversal rather than operational collapse
- Core issue remains resolution of the HPCL payment dispute and eventual monetization of the B-80 crude inventory

HEXAWARE : U.S. COURT DISMISSES MAJOR PATENT CLAIMS IN $500 MN LAWSUIT ⚖️

• 🇺🇸 U.S. District Court for the Northern District of Illinois dismissed the federal patent-related claims filed by Natsoft Corporation and Updraft LLC against Hexaware. 

• 🚫 Court held that the asserted patents relate to broad, abstract ideas and are not eligible for patent protection under U.S. law. 

• 📄 The lawsuit had originally alleged patent infringement and breach of contract, with an initial claim of USD 500 million. 

• ⚖️ Following dismissal of federal patent claims, the Court also dismissed related state-law claims, including contractual and business claims. 

• 🔬 Hexaware stated its Amaze®, Tensai® and RapidX® platforms are built on proprietary engineering and that the company itself holds U.S. patents for its technologies. 

• ⏳ Plaintiffs have been granted time to file an amended complaint with a new federal claim. 

• 💼 Company said the litigation has caused no material impact on operations, customer commitments, partner programs or financial position. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (A major portion of the $500 million litigation has been dismissed, reducing legal overhang and uncertainty for Hexaware.)

SEBI Proposes Common Pricing Rules To End Stock Price Gaps Across Exchanges

India's market regulator, the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI), has proposed a new mechanism to ensure the same stock does not trade at widely different prices across exchanges, seeking to improve price discovery and liquidity in thinly traded shares.

Under a consultation paper released Thursday, SEBI proposed that when a stock fails to trade on one exchange but trades on another, the inactive exchange should use the active exchange's closing price to determine the next day's pre-open base price and price band.

At present, exchanges independently calculate circuit limits using their own previous closing prices. In illiquid stocks, prolonged periods of non-trading on one exchange can leave prices and circuit limits frozen, even as the same stock continues to move on another platform. The divergence can eventually restrict trading and create two different market prices for the same security.

SEBI said the proposal is aimed at addressing such anomalies and ensuring uniform price bands for stocks listed on multiple exchanges.

The regulator has also proposed that where a stock trades on more than one exchange but remains inactive on another, the inactive exchange should adopt the closing price of the venue with the highest trading volume. Exchanges would be required to establish arrangements for sharing closing prices to implement the mechanism.

The changes are expected to primarily affect illiquid and small-cap stocks that do not trade regularly across all exchanges, while having little impact on actively traded securities.

Susan Electricals is showing 3 things together:

Growth + Expansion + Reasonable Valuation

The company sits in a segment where demand is not one-time.

Every grid upgrade, every old cable replacement, every reconductoring project and every push towards safer power distribution needs wires, cables, conductors and MVCC products.

That is the tailwind.

Now the company is using IPO proceeds to expand LT & HT cable capacity from 7,500 km to 12,000 km p.a.

A 60% capacity jump.

The financials also show a visible shift:

Revenue has moved from ₹103 cr to ₹269 cr
PAT has moved from ₹0.76 cr to ₹18.25 cr
EBITDA margin has improved from 3.51% to 11.91%

At the upper band, the IPO is valued at around 14x FY26 earnings and nearly 8x FY27E earnings as per working capital-linked estimates.

Anchor participation includes names like Motilal Oswal Finvest, SageOne, Shine Star Build-Cap and Arthasanchay.

Bernstein On Autos

India Autos: The 350cc battle redrawn?

Bajaj Triumph was positioned in the 400cc segment trying to build a new sub category in the premium motorcycle segment

But the GST cuts on sub-350cc motorcycles pushed Bajaj to migrate its Triumph lineup from 398cc to 349cc, placing it in the same engine segment as Royal Enfield

Royal Enfield’s demand is unambiguous - 3 month waiting periods on standard models vs Triumph is delivering within forty-eight hours

What Triumph has not yet solved is equally clear. Beyond the top 8-10 cities, it is largely absent

Royal Enfield’s grip on the Classic and Bullet buyer- rooted in community, habit, and cultural identity is not easily disrupted

ELARA CAPITAL ON INDIA PHARMA SECTOR

The U.S. Department of Defense added Wuxi AppTec to its Section 1260H "Chinese military company" list on June 8, 2026

This designation automatically labels the firm as a "Biotechnology Company of Concern" (BCC) under the BIOSECURE Act (enacted Dec 2025).

The law restricts U.S. federal agencies from procuring equipment or services from BCCs

Existing contracts signed before the designation are protected by a five-year grandfather clause, remaining valid through 2031

Wuxi AppTec denies the military links, labeling pur the designation "erroneous," and plans to pursue formal remedies to contest it

While the move is sentimentally positive for Indian CDMOs, experts warn that supply chain shifts are complex and will not provide an immediate business windfall

Bernstein On PSU Banks vs Pvt Banks

PSBs’ outperformance is now showing signs of fatigue

With 4QFY26 seeing PVBs regain earnings momentum after an extended period of weaker earnings growth.

Key drivers of PSBs’ outperformance—excess liquidity and ultra-low credit costs— are nearing exhaustion.

Funding dynamics are turning less favorable for PSBs despite continued growth outperformance.

Continue to expect a convergence in performance between the two sets of banks over the next few quarters.

Private Banks  are better positioned structurally over the longer term

PSBs- Asset quality tailwinds are peaking, with ECL impact ahead

A slowdown compared to Apr'26 (21%).

Similarly, individual policies were largely flat vs last year.

Axis Max Life (16%) o/p pvt peers (7%).

LIC's RWRP grew 15% y-y in May'26
Share of group business premiums was higher in May'26 for IPRU and HDFC Life.

SBI Life and IPRU's sum assured per ind. policy grew faster in Apr'26 (lagging data) vs peers.

JPM on HUL

OW, TP Rs 2550

Management meet takeaways,

Management reiterated a stable demand outlook, supported by calibrated pricing actions (~MSD), a resilient rural franchise, and urban premiumization as a key growth engine. 

Organizational restructuring — aimed at sharper execution across business units — together with a focused omnichannel strategy (Quick Commerce + GT specialist channels) positions HUL well to sustain its improving revenue growth trajectory.

Segment-wise, Liquids within Home Care & Personal Care, Premium Beauty & Wellbeing brands, and a scaling Foods portfolio remain the principal growth vectors.

Margins are guided within the 22.5–23.5% band for FY27, with pricing, cost efficiencies and overhead optimization absorbing 10% input cost inflation, while premiumization-led gross margin gains are expected to be reinvested into brand building.

JPM on Pharma

U.S. Department of Defense has added WuXi AppTec Co., Ltd. to its Section 1260H list of Chinese Military Companies operating in the United States

View this as sentimentally positive for Indian CRDMOs, even if the immediate business impact may be limited.

For Indian CRDMOs, key implication is that this could further support customer efforts to diversify beyond China - t his is especially relevant given India's still-small ~3.8% share of the global CRDMO market, where even modest share gains could be meaningful for leading domestic players .

Key factors to monitor are new RFQs, lateral wins, second-source qualifications, late-stage project transfers, capacity reservations, and revenue conversion

Remain positive on capability-led beneficiaries, particularly Divi’s and Anthem Biosciences .

Downgrade to reduce, TP Rs 915

Caustic soda & ECH face rising downside risk from Chinese oversupply, with China’s surplus capacity & likely export push to exert pressure on Indian prices.

Epigral is highly exposed, with 400ktpa caustic soda capacity and expanding ECH capacity, making its earnings highly sensitive to even small price declines.

With falling GM, gradual utilisation ramp-up & falling return ratios, EPS growth appears to have peaked

@Stockinfo333

Buy, TP Rs 6600

Co’s key business segment, Powergen, continues to benefit from strong industry tailwinds on both non-HHP and HHP sides.

A strong installed base, a wide product portfolio and higher geographical penetration continue to provide a stable stream of distribution revenue.

Industrial segment is growing selectively, while exports are stable across larger geographies with a little cautious approach toward the Middle Eastern markets

Expect overall EBITDA margin to remain strong on a healthy revenue mix despite higher RM prices, which are currently passed through to clients

With JSTL/TATA trading above their long-term average EV/EBITDA, high entry valuation is a key investor concern.

This inverse correlation arises as multiples expand in periods of depressed spreads, with anticipation of earning upgrades
JSTL is top Buy in metals.

Last 15-year data shows that 12-month stock returns were 15-39ppt higher when entry EV/EBITDA was above long-term average vs below.

This inverse correlation arises as multiples expand in periods of depressed spreads, with anticipation of earning upgrades.

FY27-28 EPS for JSTL/TATA are 13-36% above street.

JSW Steel is top buy in metals.

Cohance- Maintain Outperform, TP Rs 1150

Signals private companies to shift their supply chains eventually

Could meaningfully accelerate Indian CDMO market growth and market size could double in the medium term 

Remain positive on  CDMO coverage companies such as Divi's Labs, Syngene and Cohance.

Raise EPS/TP 5% each to factor in these tailwinds

See EPS tripling over FY26-29E.

The combined NMV for the three players increased 81% yoy in FY2026 compared to 84% yoy growth for Blinkit + Instamart.

Price competition remains high, with new entrants offering discounts and cash backs
Incumbents such as Blinkit seem to be more disciplined.

Near-term growth for Blinkit may revive

MORGAN STANLEY ON AI 🤖📈

• AI is still in its early stage, similar to where computers were in the 1980s and the internet was in the 1990s.

• The biggest benefits from major technologies usually come years later, after businesses fully adopt them.

• Companies are only starting to use AI to automate work, improve efficiency and make better decisions.

• While markets have already rewarded AI-related stocks, the larger productivity gains may still lie ahead.

• AI can benefit many sectors beyond technology, including healthcare, finance, manufacturing and logistics.

• If AI helps businesses produce more at lower costs, it could become a major driver of global economic growth over the coming years.

DSM FRESH FOODS (ZAPPFRESH) ANALYSIS

✅ Operates a vertically integrated farm-to-fork protein platform with presence across fresh meat, seafood and ready-to-cook/ready-to-eat foods. 

✅ Strong growth track record with revenue increasing from ₹56 Cr in FY23 to ₹221 Cr in FY26, while EBITDA margin improved from 5.5% to 14.1%. 

✅ Diversified business model with D2C, B2B, HoReCa and retail store presence. B2B now contributes 68% of revenue, improving scale and operating leverage. 

✅ Acquisitions such as Dr. Meat, Bonsaro and Ambrozia have expanded geographic reach and added high margin RTE/RTC capabilities through Meevaa Foods. 

✅ Varuna Aquatech subsidiary provides backward integration in seafood, which management expects to improve margins by 200 to 300 bps over time. 

✅ Presence in a large and largely unorganized market where nearly 90% of meat processing remains unorganized, creating significant opportunity for branded players.

The rupee’s slide against US dollar has sharpened over the past 2 years.

From Rs 83.35 per $1 in April 2024, the exchange rate moved past Rs 95 per dollar by May 2026.

At its weakest point in this period, the rupee touched Rs 96.84 per $1 on May 20, before recovering slightly to Rs 95.44 per $1 on May 26.

This means the dollar became about 16% costlier for anyone converting rupees into dollars — affecting import costs, overseas travel, foreign education payments, and companies with dollar-linked expenses.

A weaker rupee can also add pressure on India’s import bill, especially for commodities such as crude oil, while making exports relatively more competitive.

Maintain Cautious view on sector

Amidst difficult macro & rising inflationary pressures, believe consensus expectations of a mobile tariff hike in FY27 are at risk with sector revenue growth

slowing to c8% YoY in Q4FY26

Cut mobile revenue estimates for Airtel by 2-4% as now assume a c10% price increase in Q3FY27 but do not rule out risk that this gets delayed into FY28

Change in revenue estimates for VIL is smaller since recent stabilisation in subscriber base should help co accelerate 2G to 4G migration and provide upside to ARPUs

Also cut EBITDA & DPS estimates for Indus as see risk that tenancies outside of Airtel & VIL don’t get renewed while capex could remain elevated due to investments in Africa, tower relocations in India & investments in renewable energy initiatives

With stock's dividend yield at 3-5% for next 2-3 years we struggle to see compelling value

Is the Market Peaking or Entering a Super-Cycle?

Is the $1.78tn SpaceX IPO a market top or the launch of an investment super-cycle? While some fear a bubble, macro indicators suggest we are at the beginning of a massive global capex boom.

Multi-sector data shows massive acceleration. Global spending across AI, clean energy, and defense hit $6.9tn in 2025. It is projected to reach $10tn this year and scale to $16tn by 2030.

Capital is rotating into domestic infrastructure and manufacturing resilience. Reshoring trade-exposed goods entirely to the US requires $2tn in upgraded factories and facilities.

🎯 Bottom Line: With $8tn in money market funds acting as dry powder, pullbacks are tactical buying opportunities. We are entering a multi-year, capex-driven "tripolar world" expansion.
